rulings.Case Title: THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC. VS. RENE PEINADO et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2025 Line 1. PLAINTIFF THE NATIONAL COLLECTION AGENCY, INC. A CORPORATION HEARING ON CLAIM OF EXEMPTION. The hearing on Third Party Amy Ellen McDougall's Claim of Exemption is CONTINUED TO MAY 21, 2025. No later than April 24, 2025, Claimant McDougall may file a memorandum of points and authorities and supporting admissible evidence analyzing the relevant law and applying it to facts properly before the court. No later than May 1, 2025, Judgment Creditor the National Collection Agency, Inc. may file an opposition brief and supporting admissible evidence. No later than May 8, 2025, Claimant McDougall may file a reply. Filing parties must deliver to this Department a courtesy copy of any filing within 24 hours of the filing. The failure to timely provide courtesy copies may result in delay or an order taking the claim off calendar. Claimant McDougall shall forthwith lodge a proposed order with the above text and shall promptly serve the executed order on all parties entitled to notice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: CANDLESTICK HEIGHTS COMMUNITY ALLIANCE, AN
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2025 Line 2. PETITIONER CANDLESTICK HEIGHTS COMMUNITY ALLIANCE's Motion For Writ Of Mandate. The hearing on Petitioner's Motion For Writ Of Mandate is CONTINUED TO June 13, 2025. Supplemental Briefing: No later than May 22, 2025, Petitioner is to file a supplemental memorandum of points and authorities identifying what statute or statutes it is requesting writ relief under (e.g. CCP Section 1085, 1094.5), and the specific action Petitioner is challenging under that authority, the applicable legal standard, and how the record supports that relief under that standard. No later than June 2, 2025, Respondent may file an opposition brief. No later than June 6, 2025, Petitioner may file a reply. Courtesy Copies: Filing parties must deliver to this department a courtesy copy of any filing within 24 hours of the filing. The failure to timely provide courtesy copies may result in a delay or an order taking the motion off calendar. Supplemental Brief Administrative Record: Petitioner must also submit hard copies of the administrative record that is relevant to the single cause of action remaining: violation of the public trust doctrine. The administrative record must be indexed in consecutive volumes not to exceed 300 pages. The cover of each volume must identify the page range. Petitioner shall forthwith lodge by email addressed to contest email a proposed order with the above text and shall promptly serve the executed order on all parties entitled to notice. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address =(302/JMQ)

rulings.Case Title: AHMED ABDULJABBAR VS. AARON THOMPKINS ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE
rulings.Rulings: Set for Law and Motion/Discovery Calendar on Thursday, April 10, 2025 Line 5. 2 - DEFENDANTs NIKE, INC., NIKE USA, INC.,and NIKE RETAIL SERVICES, INC.'s MOTION TO ADMIT COUNSEL PRO HAC VICE. Defendants Nike Inc., Nike USA Inc., and Nike Retail Services Inc.'s unopposed Motion to Admit Counsel Colette N. Bradshears Pro Hac Vice, which is unopposed, is DENIED WITHOUT PREJUDICE. Rule 9.40 requires that counsel identify "[t]he title of each court and cause in which the applicant has filed an application to appear as counsel pro hac vice in this state in the preceding two years, the date of each application, and whether or not it was granted." (Cal. Rules of Ct., rule 9.40(d)(5).) Counsel here provides no such testimony, nor does he state he has made no such applications in the past two years. The application is denied because it is not complete. Plaintiff may file a new application with a corrected declaration.. Good cause appearing, Counsel Colette N. Bradshears may appear as counsel pro hac vice for moving parties in this action. Defendants must lodge a proposed order that includes the above text no later than 8:30 am on April 10, 2025. The proposed order shall be emailed to contestdept302tr@sftc.org. For the 9:00 a.m. calendar, all attorneys and parties may appear in Department 302 remotely or in person. Remote hearings will be conducted by videoconference using Zoom. (Dept. 302 Zoom ID 160 409 7690; Passcode 516287.) To appear remotely at the hearing, go to the court's website at sfsuperiorcourt.org under "Online Services," navigate to "Tentative Rulings," and click on the appropriate link, or dial the corresponding phone number. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The text of the email shall include the name and contact information, including email address, of the attorney or party who will appear at the hearing. Counsel for the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. The court no longer provides a court reporter in the Law & Motion Department. Parties may retain their own reporter, who may appear in the courtroom or remotely. A retained reporter must be a California certified court reporter (CSR), for only a CSR's transcript may be used in California courts. If a CSR is being retained, include in your email all of the following: their name, CSR and telephone numbers, and their individual work email address. =(302/JMQ)
